phase3_findings.description: AST substance guard verifies assertion presence, not relevance (self.assertTrue(True) would pass)
_has_real_assertion over-approximate (nested closures) and would false-FAIL a free-function assert_* helper
session_id assertions check census row, not launch provenance; comment's 'some other row's launch' wording does not hold for single-session fixtures

fix_decisions.claim_text: AST substance guard verifies presence not relevance; ast.walk descends into nested closures
fix_decisions.evidence: Statically deciding assertion relevance is undecidable; the nested-closure over-permissiveness is implausible for the current 18 owner tests (all use direct self.assert*/assert). The guard is documented as a rename+substance guard, not a semantic-relevance guard. The bare-name assert_*() acceptance added by F-2 covers the actionable false-FAIL margin.

parking_evidence.basis: inherent limit of a static substance check; relevance is not statically decidable
parking_evidence.failing_input: a mapped test reduced to self.assertTrue(True)

sweep_evidence.reason: suggestion-only fix delta; delta is test-assertion strengthening, no product/runtime-code mechanism change triggering §2.3 sweeps

phase3_findings.description: AST substance guard accepts any self.assert*, including analyzer-ran-only checks (weak-substance-guard)
COMPATIBILITY_FIXTURE_OWNERS multiplexes matrix rows onto shared tests; inventory guard does not machine-enforce that a shared test distinguishes the rows it is credited with
Several matrix rows map to tests that only assert the analyzer processes without error (runs clean), not behaves-correctly-under-condition

evidence_comparisons.rationale: shadow re-raises the inherent presence-not-relevance limit of the static substance guard at Suggestion (at/below the parked Suggestion severity), adding no new failing input
